verse_text: For by one Spirit we were all baptized into one body-whether Jews or Greeks, whether slaves or free-and have all been made to drink into aone Spirit.

links.context: Practical brotherly love is to be shown toward all Christians, but especially for the lowly, the powerless, and the victims of injustice. Strangers (v. 2) are travelers in need of hospitality. Abraham's hospitality is recalled ([[GEN.18:1]]-8; also 19:1-3). Prisoners (v. 3) are to be prayed for and aided if possible, for all those in the body of Christ, the Church, are members of one another ([[1CO.12:12]]-27).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden

links.context: Ps 80 is a psalm regarding Christ (the End) and His Church (the winepresses, v. 1). For His Church is the new wine (the new covenant), formed in the Sacrament of Baptism ([[MAT.9:16]], [[MAT.9:17]]; [[1CO.12:13]]). And Israel's deliverance from Egypt (vv. 1-11) is a type of baptism ([[1CO.10:1]], [[1CO.10:2]]). However, after baptism, the Christian is to be careful to walk in God's ways and heed His voice (vv. 12-17).

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
